discoveries_logged: D-13 §16.40 PATTERN CONFIRMED FIFTH INSTANCE — F-11 (ITTOIA 2005 s.396B vs ITA 2007 s.396B, M2-A-B3 pick 1) makes five consecutive Stage 2 statutory-cite drift catches: F-7 (s.464C/D omission, M2-A-B1) + F-8 (s.131 vs s.152, M2-A-B2) + F-9 (PM276000 vs PM250000+, M2-A-B2) + F-10 (PM236500 vs PM210000+/PM213000+, M2-A-B2) + F-11 (ITA s.396B vs ITTOIA s.396B, M2-A-B3). Three of the five are act-attribution mis-cites (s.131 vs s.152 wrong section in correct act; ITA vs ITTOIA wrong act for inserted section); two are HMRC PM range mis-attribution. The §16.36 Stage 2 statutory cross-check gate continues to earn its keep at the ~13% brief-quality error rate baseline. Recommendation: Stage 1 dispatch prompt should explicitly enumerate the known-drift watchpoints (s.464C/D omitted; s.131 routes via s.152; PM276000 = CIS not salaried-member; PM236500 does not exist; ITTOIA 2005 s.396B not ITA 2007) as a worked-example of what verification catches.

D-14 ALL SIX BRIEF EXTENSIONS THIS BATCH ANCHOR TO THE LIVE §11.C CLUSTER (PARENT + 3 SUB-LOCKS) + §11.B LP REFORMS — F-2 / F-4 / F-5 / F-6 lock requests raised at MW2 Stage 1 (across M2-A-B2 and M2-A-B3) have all been APPROVED and locked between Stage 1 and Stage 2. The pre-Stage-2 lock approval pattern is the operational baseline that drives the §16.46 zero-Q&A observation forward.

D-15 ENTITY-CHOICE DECISION-FRAME COHERENCE ACROSS BUCKET — Bucket A's six picks now span the complete LtdCo + partnership decision frame: (i) the entity-types comparison (`sole-trader-vs-partnership` early fork); (ii) the partnership architecture (`partnership-partnership-agreement-roles-types-benefits` mid-fork); (iii) the LLP accounts + tax handbooks (`llp-accounts` + `llp-and-taxation-benefits`); (iv) the LtdCo entity pillar (A20 + A1 + this batch's `limited-companies-and-btl-properties` operational BTL handbook); (v) the CT-registration entry-point (`register-for-uk-corporation-tax`). Cross-link grid is dense and reciprocal. RUN-session writers across the six picks should rely on the sibling forward-links rather than restating sub-regime depth.

D-16 STAGE-2 PER-WRITE VERIFICATION RISK FOR THE BATCH — LtdCo and partnership clusters carry several rate-by-reference figures that RUN session must WebFetch at write time per §16.35 + §16.27: dividend rates (10.75% / 35.75% / 39.35% from 6 April 2026 per F-20 §21.4); s.455 rate (35.75% post-6-April-2026 per F-9 §21.1; 33.75% pre); secondary Class 1 NIC rate (15% from 6 April 2026 per F-19 §21.4); CGT residential rates (24% post-FA 2024 s.10; verify current FA 2025 + FA 2026 calibration); CGT AEA (£3k FY26 — verify current); FRS 105 + FRS 102 + LLP SORP current published versions; audit-exemption thresholds (post 6-April-2025 uplift); HMRC official rate of interest on BIK calculation; FA 2008 Sch 41 penalty calibration; TMA 1970 s.86 interest rate. The brief framework holds the architecture; the numbers are commodity.
anti_templating_check: Six briefs read as a set. Boilerplate sections (Voice + style, 19-step workflow, Quality bar six checks) verbatim per §4.8 + §7. Substantive content (framing differentiators, worked examples, FAQ phrasing, statutory-anchor depth) is distinct per pick. Each pick's worked-example section uses distinct personas (Singh / Kapoor / Mawell-Estate / Patel / Verma / Singh-Family / Mr Singh-business-associate cycled across picks with role-variation to avoid template feel; no two picks reuse identical persona-role pairings). FAQ phrasing varies; do-not-write GREP discipline patterns vary per pick (10-14 patterns per pick reflecting the specific anti-avoidance regimes + house-position locks each pick touches). Worked-example arithmetic varies per pick (FRS 105 vs FRS 102 comparative on £300k portfolio; DLA-IN/OUT flip on £50k; £500k portfolio Form 17 vs partnership; £400k partnership 50/50 s.24(1) disaster; £2m partnership-to-LtdCo Sch 15 SLP; £1.5m partnership s.26 dissolution; £3m 4-SPV group operation). No template-feel; each worked-example holds the pick's distinctive operational angle.
